title: uropappus lindleyi
intro: Uropappus lindleyi, also known as Lindley's horseweed or Fraser's horseweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Uropappus lindleyi include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
